channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Netanyahu scolds Israeli security minister for videos taunting flotilla activists
item.description: Israel's national security minister triggered a backlash after releasing videos taunting detained flotilla activists who tried to breach the blockade of Gaza, telling them they should be imprisoned.
item.content:encoded:
Israel's national security minister triggered a backlash after releasing videos taunting detained flotilla activists who tried to breach the blockade of Gaza, telling them they should be imprisoned.

(Image credit: Ohad Zwigenberg)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Ebola fears surge on the ground in Congo over rapid spread of a rare type
item.description: Healthcare workers in eastern Congo said Wednesday they are underprotected and undertrained in a rapidly spreading Ebola outbreak of a rare type of the virus in one of the world's most vulnerable places.
item.content:encoded:
Healthcare workers in eastern Congo said Wednesday they are underprotected and undertrained in a rapidly spreading Ebola outbreak of a rare type of the virus in one of the world's most vulnerable places.

(Image credit: Moses Sawasawa)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Liberal U.S. mayors team up with European counterparts to fight authoritarianism
item.description: Ten U.S. mayors from cities such as Chicago and Cincinnati have joined a pact with European mayors to defend democracy and progressive values and fight right-wing populists and authoritarianism.
item.content:encoded:
Ten U.S. mayors from cities such as Chicago and Cincinnati have joined a pact with European mayors to defend democracy and progressive values and fight right-wing populists and authoritarianism.

(Image credit: Courtesy of the city of Bratislava)
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt

item.title: Cuba's Raúl Castro indicted over 1996 fatal downing of two aircraft
item.description: Miami prosecutors have prepared an indictment against former Cuban president Raúl Castro over the 1996 downing of two NGO planes that killed four people onboard.
item.content:encoded: Miami prosecutors have prepared an indictment against former Cuban president Raúl Castro over the 1996 downing of two NGO planes that killed four people onboard.
item.dc:creator: Eyder Peralta

item.title: The U.S. threatens to revoke the Palestinian U.N. ambassador's visa
item.description: A leaked State Department memo shows the U.S. threatened to revoke Palestinian visas if they pushed for a senior United Nations post.
item.content:encoded:
A leaked State Department memo shows the U.S. threatened to revoke Palestinian visas if they pushed for a senior United Nations post.

(Image credit: Angelina Katsanis)
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: U.S. grand jury indicts Raúl Castro, former Cuban president
item.description: The 94-year-old former leader of Cuba faces several charges, including four counts of murder for an attack on a humanitarian group more than 30 years ago.
item.content:encoded:
The 94-year-old former leader of Cuba faces several charges, including four counts of murder for an attack on a humanitarian group more than 30 years ago.

(Image credit: Alexandre Meneghini)
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz

item.title: Greetings from Bali, where a kecak dance shows the triumph of good over evil
item.description: The kecak dance involves a retelling of one of the stories in the Ramayana, the Hindu epic poem. At the story's climax, there is an eruption of fire as tufts of dried coconut husks are set aflame.
item.content:encoded: The kecak dance involves a retelling of one of the stories in the Ramayana, the Hindu epic poem. At the story's climax, there is an eruption of fire as tufts of dried coconut husks are set aflame.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: Takeaways from Tuesday's primaries. And, victims of mosque shooting revealed
item.description: Trump-backed Ed Gallrein ousted Congressman Thomas Massie in Kentucky's Republican House primary. And, police have identified the victims in California's mosque shooting.
item.content:encoded:
Trump-backed Ed Gallrein ousted Congressman Thomas Massie in Kentucky's Republican House primary. And, police have identified the victims in California's mosque shooting.

(Image credit: Jon Cherry)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Former Spanish Prime Minister Zapatero is under investigation
item.description: A Spanish court is investigating former Prime Minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero for alleged influence peddling and other possible crimes tied to a government airline bailout.
item.content:encoded:
A Spanish court is investigating former Prime Minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero for alleged influence peddling and other possible crimes tied to a government airline bailout.

(Image credit: Juan Karita)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Bees have coexisted with us for over a millennium. Their name remains a mystery
item.description: Its name is short — like its size — but the bee is one of Earth's most important and busy creatures.
item.content:encoded:
Its name is short — like its size — but the bee is one of Earth's most important and busy creatures.

(Image credit: Alan Nakkash for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim
